patch.value: CodeFactor remediation: drive code quality from A to A+ (zero outstanding findings)

Track and eliminate every CodeFactor finding so the repository reaches the best attainable rating with no outstanding issues.

## Source

- CodeFactor overview: https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/unbraind/pm-cli/overview/main
- Issues: https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/main
- Files by rating: https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/unbraind/pm-cli/files/main?grid-column=Rating&grid-dir=0

CodeFactor was wired up alongside Codecov in commit `d42ae71b` (README badge + `.codefactor.yml` scoping analysis to hand-authored source, excluding `.agents/**`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `PRD.md`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`).

## Snapshot (captured 2026-06-26)

- Overall grade: **A** (98.7% A-rated files, 1.0% B, 0.3% C)
- Files analyzed: 601 · Active branch: main
- **Total outstanding issues: 261**

| Category | Count | Severity / rule split |
|---|---|---|
| Complexity | 152 | Critical 3 · High 7 · Moderate 142 |
| Maintainability | 89 | `no-useless-fallback-in-spread` 57 · unsafe optional chaining 25 · `no-thenable` 4 · `no-useless-spread` 2 · `no-useless-length-check` 1 |
| Duplication | 20 | Critical 2 · Major 3 · Minor 15 |

## Hotspot files (lowest-rated)

1. `src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts` (B) — includes the `no-thenable` findings (`then:` keys at lines 2483, 2493)
2. `tests/unit/commands/create-command.spec.ts` (C)
3. `tests/unit/commands/update-command.spec.ts` (B)
4. `src/cli/commands/search.ts` (B)
5. `src/cli/main.ts` (B)

## Approach

Child items break the work down by category and rule. Order of attack:
1. Maintainability — mechanical, low-risk, auto-fixable ESLint rules (fastest grade wins).
2. Duplication — extract shared helpers (Critical/Major first).
3. Complexity — refactor the worst functions (Critical/High first), then chip away at Moderate.
4. Stand up local lint/quality parity so the team can reach zero and prevent regressions.

NOTE: CodeFactor analyzes its own snapshot; `.agents/**` (the git-native work-item store, where these very items live), `CHANGELOG.md`, `PRD.md`, and the lockfile are excluded by `.codefactor.yml`, so these tracking items do not themselves add findings.

CodeFactor overall repository grade reaches A+ (or the highest attainable) on branch main; CodeFactor reports 0 outstanding issues across Complexity, Maintainability, and Duplication; local lint/quality parity tooling exists so findings can be reproduced and kept at zero (no regressions on new PRs).

Fixed the generated Codex MCP wrapper by restoring explicit return null after the depth-limited repo search and added a focused plugin wrapper test that exercises that branch. value.text: Second Greptile response evidence: amended review 097e49c1-165e-478e-bcde-8e7552c52070 cleared the prior P1/P2 and reported one P2 startup inefficiency: pm-codex wrapper eagerly evaluated repoServerPath even with PM_CLI_MCP_SERVER set. Fixed the generator so the Codex wrapper uses startReadableServer and short-circuits env path before repo discovery, matching the pm-claude behavior. value.text: Greptile CJS-global follow-up evidence: local greptile review --branch main --json on head 8d6308af returned one P2 finding that NODE_GLOBALS exposed CommonJS-only identifiers to .mjs lint, hiding require/module/__dirname runtime mistakes. Fixed by removing __dirname, __filename, require, module, and exports from shared NODE_GLOBALS, scoping them to files: ["**/*.cjs"] through COMMONJS_GLOBALS, and adding release-automation contract coverage that keeps CommonJS globals out of the ESM lint surface. Verification passed: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/release-automation-contract.spec.ts (1 file / 16 tests); pnpm typecheck; pnpm lint with ESLint clean, jscpd 0 clones across 551 files, static quality passed; pnpm security:scan; pnpm audit --audit-level moderate; git diff --check; full node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage (264 files / 5327 tests / 100% statements, branches, functions, and lines).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Greptile final follow-up evidence: local greptile review --branch main --json on head 9bb54da1 returned two P2 findings: the underscore unused-variable convention omitted future .js lint surfaces, and inline pragma budget scanning read every file once per pattern. Fixed both by widening the ESLint override to files: ["**/*.{js,mjs,ts}"], adding release-contract coverage for that pattern, pre-reading pragma scan files once with readPragmaScanTexts, reusing cached scan text for each budget pattern, and collapsing unreadable scan files into one inline_pragmas scan-level error. Removed the obsolete per-budget error branch after coverage exposed it as unreachable. Verification passed: focused static-quality/release-contract suite (2 files / 56 tests); pnpm typecheck; pnpm lint with ESLint clean, jscpd 0 clones across 551 files, static quality passed; pnpm security:scan; pnpm audit --audit-level moderate; git diff --check; full node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage (264 files / 5328 tests / 100% statements, branches, functions, and lines).

patch.value: CodeFactor overall repository grade reaches A+ (or the highest attainable) on branch main; CodeFactor reports 0 outstanding issues across Complexity, Maintainability, and Duplication; local lint/quality parity tooling exists so findings can be reproduced and kept at zero (no regressions on new PRs).

## Charter
Quality epic: drive CodeFactor from A to A+ and keep it there. eslint-suppressions.json (max 17/file rule budget) IS the CodeFactor source of truth; suppressions ratcheted 168 -> 109 (2026-07-04). Complexity: `?.` counts toward CC; static complexity cap 260; LOC cap 3400 per file.

## Method
- Work from the live findings list (see Source links in description), never from memory.
- Decompose god-functions by extracting phases at the same call position to preserve behavior/order (PR#412 runCreate 172->≤17 precedent).
- Duplication: jscpd gate (.jscpd.json minTokens 115; ratchet toward ~90 as clones are paid down).

## Open children (2026-07-04)
- pm-jt3b — split search.ts (3119 LOC, 92% of cap) into rendering/query-parsing/inline-field modules.
- pm-ueuq — dedupe flag-contracts.ts per-command clone blocks (~150 lines) + cross-file clone pairs.
- pm-f4yn — prune commands barrel dead re-exports; adopt knip as a dead-export gate.
Also related (not a child): pm-0n6p runUpdate/mutate CC follow-up.

## Exit criteria
CodeFactor overview shows A+ with zero outstanding findings; suppression count stable or falling; all three children closed with gate evidence.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Gate-calibration discovery (2026-07-12, GH-518/pm-2gvp): CodeFactor's "Complex Method" detector is NESTING-WEIGHTED, not plain cyclomatic. Evidence: it flagged renderNextMarkdown (cyclomatic 14, cognitive 22) while never flagging runNext in the same file (cyclomatic 16, cognitive 5 — flat early-return style). The old `complexity max 17` gate was calibrated to a "CC>=18" assumption and was structurally blind to this class. Fix: twin ceilings — complexity max 16 + sonarjs/cognitive-complexity max 16 (user policy: nothing over 16 on either metric); 76 pre-existing violations grandfathered (suppressions 104->180, budget re-pinned, burn-down = pm-j8vq). Any future CodeFactor Complex Method finding on code that passes both ceilings means the calibration needs ANOTHER metric — re-derive from the flagged function's scores, do not bump thresholds blindly.

value.text: Implementation evidence: moved the mandatory static-quality gate from .mjs to .mts and execute it through the existing tsx development dependency. Every import, contract, suppression-baseline path, and maintainer reference follows the move; the gate still enforces exactly 154 ESLint suppressions, 100% docstrings, zero boundary drift, zero unsupported dynamic imports, and all token/package gates. Added extension-aware Vite harness coverage for transformed .mts imports. Focused quality/release suite passes 115/115; pnpm typecheck, lint, contracts, security, and diff checks pass.

value.author: codex-gpt5-root
value.text: Coverage-integrity correction: the first full run was green but revealed that the renamed .mts gate fell outside the prior scripts/**/*.mjs include glob, reducing measured totals. Added scripts/*.mts and scripts/**/*.mts to literal all-source coverage, extended the cross-platform shebang transform, included .mts in pragma scanning, and strengthened the release manifest plus scan regressions. Focused 106-test verification and the complete strict lint/static/docstring/package/token gate pass; rerunning the entire coverage corpus now with the gate restored to scope.

The .mts static gate is included and individually reaches 100% after replacing two unreachable fallback pragmas with structural invariants and exercising the destructured-export branch.

value.author: codex-gpt5-root
value.text: Root-cause evidence: DeepSource run 51a75878-29de-4498-bdf7-b1c7173480a2 reports 1,414 NEW JavaScript findings across 36 PR files, including 125 in src/sdk/query/list.ts, while its live metadata says dialect=typescript but module_system=commonjs, style_guide=google, and complexity=low. This contradicts the committed ESM TOML and produces false global-function/doc/complexity findings. Official DeepSource analyzer metadata is now explicit for dialect=typescript, environment=nodejs+vitest, module_system=es-modules, and complexity=high; the repo's local complexity/docstring gates remain stricter.
